2180.reason: The loop condition `tokenList.get(i).line.equals(nowToken.line)` may cause an infinite loop or incorrect behavior when the current token's line changes, and it does not properly handle the case where the next token is on a different line (e.g., after a comment or newline). This leads to failure in detecting missing semicolons (error i) because the loop may not find the ASSIGN token on the same line, causing the parser to incorrectly treat the statement as an expression statement instead of an assignment statement.

1950.reason: The parser expects a comma if the next token is not a semicolon. If a semicolon is missing (error 'i'), the next token might be a keyword or identifier from the next statement. getToken(COMMA) will fail and throw a RuntimeException because COMMA is not handled in the error recovery switch case, causing the program to crash before writing errors to file.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The parser expects a comma if the next token is not a semicolon. If a semicolon is missing (error 'i'), the next token might be a keyword or identifier from the next statement. getToken(COMMA) will fail and throw a RuntimeException because COMMA is not handled in the error recovery switch case, causing the program to crash before writing errors to file.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  Similar to ConstDecl, if a semicolon is missing (error i), the next token is not a comma. This causes getToken(COMMA) to crash with a RuntimeException instead of detecting the missing semicolon and allowing the parser to continue or report the error correctly.
